text:
My idea of a good time is working with amazing engineers to create incredible technology 🤩

The Tesla chip research fab will have all the machines needed to do logic, memory, packing & masks in one building for a lightning fast development cycle. Heaven 💫
summary: My idea of a good time is working with amazing engineers to create incredible technology 🤩 The Tesla chip research fab will have all the machines needed to do logic, memory, packing & masks in one building for a lightning fast development cycle. Heaven 💫
ai_summary: Elon Musk discusses Tesla's vertically integrated chip research and manufacturing facility, which consolidates logic design, memory, packaging, and mask production under one roof to accelerate development cycles. The insight emphasizes how collocating all semiconductor manufacturing steps in a single facility eliminates handoffs and communication delays, enabling faster iteration—a key operational strategy for companies building custom silicon at scale.
embed_html:
My idea of a good time is working with amazing engineers to create incredible technology 🤩

The Tesla chip research fab will have all the machines needed to do logic, memory, packing & masks in one building for a lightning fast development cycle. Heaven 💫

— Elon Musk (@elonmusk) April 4, 2026

text: Grok is constantly being updated, so there is a good chance that what didn’t work for you even a few days ago might work now https://t.co/Uvg1bEgNiy
summary: Grok is constantly being updated, so there is a good chance that what didn’t work for you even a few days ago might work now
ai_summary: Elon Musk announces that Grok, xAI's conversational AI assistant, is undergoing continuous updates and improvements, implying that users experiencing issues or limitations should retry their queries as the product's capabilities are rapidly evolving. This reflects a common product development strategy where AI systems are iterated upon frequently, and serves as a business insight about managing user expectations during the rapid deployment and refinement phase of AI products.
embed_html:
Grok is constantly being updated, so there is a good chance that what didn’t work for you even a few days ago might work now https://t.co/Uvg1bEgNiy

— Elon Musk (@elonmusk) April 3, 2026

text: Tesla cars, especially with FSD, are the safest in the world https://t.co/1TPXVeUnb6
summary: Tesla cars, especially with FSD, are the safest in the world
ai_summary: Elon Musk asserts that Tesla vehicles, particularly those equipped with Full Self-Driving (FSD) capability, represent the safest cars globally. This directly relates to Tesla's core business strategy of developing and commercializing autonomous driving technology as a key competitive differentiator and value proposition in the automotive market.
embed_html:
Tesla cars, especially with FSD, are the safest in the world https://t.co/1TPXVeUnb6

— Elon Musk (@elonmusk) April 3, 2026

ai_summary: Elon Musk promotes Grok, xAI's conversational AI assistant, by sharing a direct link for users to try the product. This represents a product launch/adoption push for xAI's flagship AI offering, demonstrating how founders directly drive user acquisition and engagement for their technology products in the early growth phase.
embed_html:
Try Grok https://t.co/R0YRR3UsgL

text: Using voice for Imagine is a great feature for young kids who can talk and have amazing imagination, but are too young to write complex prompts https://t.co/CpY6zup1rJ
summary: Using voice for Imagine is a great feature for young kids who can talk and have amazing imagination, but are too young to write complex prompts
ai_summary: Elon discusses a voice input feature for Grok's Imagine (image generation tool), highlighting how voice prompts lower the barrier to entry for younger users who lack written communication skills. This reflects a product design decision that expands addressable user base by making complex AI interactions more accessible through natural language input rather than text-based prompts.
embed_html:
Using voice for Imagine is a great feature for young kids who can talk and have amazing imagination, but are too young to write complex prompts https://t.co/CpY6zup1rJ

— Elon Musk (@elonmusk) April 3, 2026

text: Grok can help you come up with great prompts for images and videos https://t.co/ToWtLoAqUs
summary: Grok can help you come up with great prompts for images and videos
ai_summary: Elon Musk announces that Grok, xAI's AI assistant, now has the capability to help users generate effective prompts for image and video creation tools. This demonstrates Grok's expanding feature set and practical applications as a productivity tool that enhances user workflows by improving prompt engineering—a key skill in maximizing AI model outputs across generative media use cases.
embed_html:
Grok can help you come up with great prompts for images and videos https://t.co/ToWtLoAqUs

— Elon Musk (@elonmusk) April 3, 2026

text: @deaflibertarian I am confident that Neuralink will restore hearing one day, just as we will restore vision with our Blindsight implant
summary: @deaflibertarian I am confident that Neuralink will restore hearing one day, just as we will restore vision with our Blindsight implant

More details to come!

ai_summary: SpaceX staff demonstrated care toward deaf children, prompting Elon to highlight Neuralink's medical technology roadmap. The insight reflects how Neuralink is positioning itself as a serious biotech venture aimed at restoring sensory capabilities (hearing via neural implants, vision via Blindsight), revealing the company's long-term commercialization strategy beyond neural interfaces for able-bodied users.

— Elon Musk (@elonmusk) April 4, 2026

parent.text: David Friedberg today on @Tesla: "I think we're going to look back one day and have this laughing observation that Tesla started out as an electric car company, ended up becoming an autonomous car company, and the autonomous competency lead to the robotics revolution. Even if
parent.name: Sawyer Merritt
parent.embed_html:
David Friedberg today on @Tesla:

"I think we're going to look back one day and have this laughing observation that Tesla started out as an electric car company, ended up becoming an autonomous car company, and the autonomous competency lead to the robotics revolution. Even if… pic.twitter.com/mmCxGSstBY

— Sawyer Merritt (@SawyerMerritt) April 4, 2026

ai_summary: David Friedberg observes that Tesla's evolution may follow a trajectory from electric vehicles to autonomous driving to robotics, with autonomous competency being the crucial bridge enabling a broader robotics revolution. Elon's affirmation signals this strategic pivot is plausible, highlighting how specialized technical mastery in one domain (autonomous systems) can become a foundational capability for entering and dominating adjacent markets with massive TAM potential.
embed_html:
Might actually happen

— Elon Musk (@elonmusk) April 4, 2026

text:

summary: @kimbal Thanks, bro. So much love was put into that product.

parent.text: The best car in 100 years. It changed the game by being the best car, period. It moved the world towards EV’s at scale and did more for the environment than any other product of any kind (until the Model 3 and Y came out). Nice work, Bro.
